name: s3path

metadata.name: s3path
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# S3Path ```{image} https://badgen.net/pypi/v/s3path :alt: Latest version :target: https://pypi.org/project/s3path/ ``` ```{image} https://github.com/liormizr/s3path/actions/workflows/testing.yml/badge.svg?branch=master&event=push :alt: S3Path CI :target: https://github.com/liormizr/s3path/actions/workflows/testing.yml ``` S3Path provide a Python convenient File-System/Path like interface for AWS S3 Service using boto3 S3 resource as a driver. ## Like pathlib, but for S3 Buckets AWS S3 is among the most popular cloud storage solutions. It's object storage, is built to store and retrieve various amounts of data from anywhere. Currently, Python developers use Boto3 as the default API to connect / put / get / list / delete files from S3. S3Path blends Boto3's ease of use and the familiarity of pathlib api. # Install: From PyPI: ```bash $ pip install s3path ``` From Conda: ```bash $ conda install -c conda-forge s3path ``` # Basic use: The following example assumes an s3 bucket setup as specified bellow: ```bash $ aws s3 ls s3://pypi-proxy/ 2018-04-24 22:59:59 186 requests/index.html 2018-04-24 22:59:57 485015 requests/requests-2.9.1.tar.gz 2018-04-24 22:35:01 89112 boto3/boto3-1.4.1.tar.gz 2018-04-24 22:35:02 180 boto3/index.html 2018-04-24 22:35:19 3308919 botocore/botocore-1.4.93.tar.gz 2018-04-24 22:35:36 188 botocore/index.html ``` Importing the main class: ```python >>> from s3path import S3Path ``` Listing "subdirectories" - s3 keys can be split like file-system with a `/` in s3path we: ```python >>> bucket_path = S3Path('/pypi-proxy/') >>> [path for path in bucket_path.iterdir() if path.is_dir()] [S3Path('/pypi-proxy/requests/'), S3Path('/pypi-proxy/boto3/'), S3Path('/pypi-proxy/botocore/')] ``` Listing html source files in this "directory" tree: ```python >>> bucket_path = S3Path('/pypi-proxy/') >>> list(bucket_path.glob('**/*.html')) [S3Path('/pypi-proxy/requests/index.html'), S3Path('/pypi-proxy/boto3/index.html'), S3Path('/pypi-proxy/botocore/index.html')] ``` Navigating inside a "directory" tree: ```python >>> bucket_path = S3Path('/pypi-proxy/') >>> boto3_package_path = bucket_path / 'boto3' / 'boto3-1.4.1.tar.gz' >>> boto3_package_path S3Path('/pypi-proxy/boto3/boto3-1.4.1.tar.gz') ``` Querying path properties: ```python >>> boto3_package_path = S3Path('/pypi-proxy/boto3/boto3-1.4.1.tar.gz') >>> boto3_package_path.exists() True >>> boto3_package_path.is_dir() False >>> boto3_package_path.is_file() True ``` Opening a "file" (s3 key): ```python >>> botocore_index_path = S3Path('/pypi-proxy/botocore/index.html') >>> with botocore_index_path.open() as f: >>> print(f.read()) """ Package Index botocore-1.4.93.tar.gz
""" ``` Or Simply reading: ```python >>> botocore_index_path = S3Path('/pypi-proxy/botocore/index.html') >>> botocore_index_path.read_text() """ Package Index botocore-1.4.93.tar.gz
""" ``` # Versioned S3 Objects: s3path supports versioned objects for S3 buckets that have versioning enabled. `VersionedS3Path` is a subclass of `S3Path` that supports all of its features. The main difference is an additional required `version_id` keyword parameter in each of its constructor methods. ```python >>> from s3path import VersionedS3Path >>> bucket, key, version_id = 'my-bucket', 'my-key', 'my-version-id' >>> VersionedS3Path(f'/{bucket}/{key}', version_id=version_id) VersionedS3Path('/my-bucket/my-key', version_id='my-version-id') >>> VersionedS3Path.from_uri(f's3://{bucket}/{key}', version_id=version_id) VersionedS3Path('/my-bucket/my-key', version_id='my-version-id') >>> VersionedS3Path.from_bucket_key(bucket=bucket, key=key, version_id=version_id) VersionedS3Path('/my-bucket/my-key', version_id='my-version-id') ``` New in version 0.5.0 # Requirements: - Python >= 3.4 - boto3 - smart-open # Further Documentation: - [Advanced S3Path configuration] (S3 parameters, S3-compatible storage, etc.) - [Abstract pathlib interface] implemented by S3Path - [Boto3 vs S3Path usage examples] [abstract pathlib interface]: https://github.com/liormizr/s3path/blob/master/docs/interface.rst [advanced s3path configuration]: https://github.com/liormizr/s3path/blob/master/docs/advance.rst [boto3 vs s3path usage examples]: https://github.com/liormizr/s3path/blob/master/docs/comparison.rst

modules.name: s3path
documentation: s3path provides a Pythonic API to S3 by wrapping boto3 with pathlib interface

classes.name: s3path.current_version.PureS3Path

methods.name: s3path.current_version.PureS3Path.from_uri
documentation:
from_uri class method create a class instance from url

>> from s3path import PureS3Path
>> PureS3Path.from_uri('s3://<bucket>/<key>')
<< PureS3Path('/<bucket>/<key>')

methods.name: s3path.current_version.PureS3Path.from_bucket_key
documentation:
from_bucket_key class method create a class instance from bucket, key pair's

>> from s3path import PureS3Path
>> PureS3Path.from_bucket_key(bucket='<bucket>', key='<key>')
<< PureS3Path('/<bucket>/<key>')

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.stat
documentation:
Returns information about this path (similarly to boto3's ObjectSummary).
For compatibility with pathlib, the returned object some similar attributes like os.stat_result.
The result is looked up at each call to this method

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.absolute
documentation:
Handle absolute method only if the path is already an absolute one
since we have no way to compute an absolute path from a relative one in S3.

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.owner
documentation:
Returns the name of the user owning the Bucket or key.
Similarly to boto3's ObjectSummary owner attribute

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.rename
documentation:
Renames this file or Bucket / key prefix / key to the given target.
If target exists and is a file, it will be replaced silently if the user has permission.
If path is a key prefix, it will replace all the keys with the same prefix to the new target prefix.
Target can be either a string or another S3Path object.

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.replace
documentation:
Renames this Bucket / key prefix / key to the given target.
If target points to an existing Bucket / key prefix / key, it will be unconditionally replaced.

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.rmdir
documentation: Removes this Bucket / key prefix. The Bucket / key prefix must be empty

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.samefile
documentation:
Returns whether this path points to the same Bucket key as other_path,
Which can be either a Path object, or a string

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.touch
documentation:
Creates a key at this given path.
If the key already exists,
the function succeeds if exist_ok is true (and its modification time is updated to the current time),
otherwise FileExistsError is raised

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.mkdir
documentation:
Create a path bucket.
AWS S3 Service doesn't support folders, therefore the mkdir method will only create the current bucket.
If the bucket path already exists, FileExistsError is raised.

If exist_ok is false (the default), FileExistsError is raised if the target Bucket already exists.
If exist_ok is true, OSError exceptions will be ignored.

if parents is false (the default), mkdir will create the bucket only if this is a Bucket path.
if parents is true, mkdir will create the bucket even if the path have a Key path.

mode argument is ignored.

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.is_dir
documentation:
Returns True if the path points to a Bucket or a key prefix, False if it points to a full key path.
False is also returned if the path doesn’t exist.
Other errors (such as permission errors) are propagated.

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.is_file
documentation:
Returns True if the path points to a Bucket key, False if it points to Bucket or a key prefix.
False is also returned if the path doesn’t exist.
Other errors (such as permission errors) are propagated.

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.exists
documentation: Whether the path points to an existing Bucket, key or key prefix.

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.iterdir
documentation: When the path points to a Bucket or a key prefix, yield path objects of the directory contents

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.open
documentation: Opens the Bucket key pointed to by the path, returns a Key file object that you can read/write with

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.glob
documentation:
Glob the given relative pattern in the Bucket / key prefix represented by this path,
yielding all matching files (of any kind)

The glob method is using a new Algorithm that better fit S3 API

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.rglob
documentation:
This is like calling S3Path.glob with "**/" added in front of the given relative pattern

The rglob method is using a new Algorithm that better fit S3 API

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.get_presigned_url
documentation:
Returns a pre-signed url. Anyone with the url can make a GET request to get the file.
You can set an expiration date with the expire_in argument (integer or timedelta object).

Note that generating a presigned url may require more information or setup than to use other
S3Path functions. It's because it needs to know the exact aws region and use s3v4 as signature
version. Meaning you may have to do this:

```python
import boto3
from botocore.config import Config
from s3path import S3Path, register_configuration_parameter

resource = boto3.resource(
    "s3",
    config=Config(signature_version="s3v4"),
    region_name="the aws region name"
)
register_configuration_parameter(S3Path("/"), resource=resource)
```

A simple example:
```python
from s3path import S3Path
import requests

file = S3Path("/my-bucket/toto.txt")
file.write_text("hello world")

presigned_url = file.get_presigned_url()
print(requests.get(presigned_url).content)
b"hello world"

methods.name: s3path.current_version.S3Path.unlink
documentation: Remove this key from its bucket.

methods.name: s3path.current_version.PureVersionedS3Path.from_uri
documentation:
from_uri class method creates a class instance from uri and version id

>> from s3path import VersionedS3Path
>> VersionedS3Path.from_uri('s3://<bucket>/<key>', version_id='<version_id>')
<< VersionedS3Path('/<bucket>/<key>', version_id='<version_id>')

methods.name: s3path.current_version.PureVersionedS3Path.from_bucket_key
documentation:
from_bucket_key class method creates a class instance from bucket, key and version id

>> from s3path import VersionedS3Path
>> VersionedS3Path.from_bucket_key('<bucket>', '<key>', version_id='<version_id>')
<< VersionedS3Path('/<bucket>/<key>', version_id='<version_id>')

methods.name: s3path.current_version.PureVersionedS3Path.with_segments
documentation:
Construct a new path object from any number of path-like objects.
Subclasses may override this method to customize how new path objects
are created from methods like `iterdir()`.

PurePath subclass for AWS S3 service Keys with Versions.

S3 is not a file-system, but we can look at it like a POSIX system.

classes.name: s3path.current_version.VersionedS3Path
documentation:

<< VersionedS3Path('/<bucket>/<key>', version_id='<version_id>')

classes.name: s3path.accessor.StatResult
documentation: Base of os.stat_result but with boto3 s3 features
For Development on Cli, or in general application that require fast startup
This will lazy load boto3 resources
boto3 increase startup time by X10!

methods.name: s3path.old_versions.S3Path.mkdir
documentation:
Create a path bucket.
AWS S3 Service doesn't support folders, therefore the mkdir method will only create the current bucket.
If the bucket path already exists, FileExistsError is raised.

If exist_ok is false (the default), FileExistsError is raised if the target Bucket already exists.
If exist_ok is true, OSError exceptions will be ignored.

if parents is false (the default), mkdir will create the bucket only if this is a Bucket path.
if parents is true, mkdir will create the bucket even if the path have a Key path.

mode argument is ignored.

methods.name: s3path.old_versions.S3Path.get_presigned_url
documentation:
Returns a pre-signed url. Anyone with the url can make a GET request to get the file.
You can set an expiration date with the expire_in argument (integer or timedelta object).

Note that generating a presigned url may require more information or setup than to use other
S3Path functions. It's because it needs to know the exact aws region and use s3v4 as signature
version. Meaning you may have to do this:

```python
import boto3
from botocore.config import Config
from s3path import S3Path, register_configuration_parameter

resource = boto3.resource(
    "s3",
    config=Config(signature_version="s3v4"),
    region_name="the aws region name"
)
register_configuration_parameter(S3Path("/"), resource=resource)
```

A simple example:
```python
from s3path import S3Path
import requests

file = S3Path("/my-bucket/toto.txt")
file.write_text("hello world")

presigned_url = file.get_presigned_url()
print(requests.get(presigned_url).content)
b"hello world"

Path subclass for AWS S3 service.

S3Path provide a Python convenient File-System/Path like interface for AWS S3 Service
 using boto3 S3 resource as a driver.

If boto3 isn't installed in your environment NotImplementedError will be raised.

from_bucket_key class method creates a class instance from bucket, key and version id

>> from s3path import VersionedS3Path
>> VersionedS3Path.from_bucket_key('<bucket>', '<key>', version_id='<version_id>')
<< VersionedS3Path('/<bucket>/<key>', version_id='<version_id>')

PurePath subclass for AWS S3 service Keys with Versions.

S3 is not a file-system, but we can look at it like a POSIX system.
